task_completion.description: Open the fin configuration dialog in OpenRocket.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenRocket application window with the fin configuration dialog box open for the 'A simple model rocket' design.

task_completion.description: Modify the fins to have a 3.0-degree cant angle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the fin configuration dialog in OpenRocket. The 'Cant angle' field should be set to 3.0 degrees (values between 2.5 and 3.5 are acceptable).

task_completion.description: Run a flight simulation to determine the apogee and performance impacts.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Flight simulations' tab in OpenRocket showing a recently run simulation with flight data (like apogee) visible.

task_completion.description: Save the modified rocket design to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a save dialog, file manager, or the OpenRocket window title bar indicating the file is saved as 'spin_stabilized_rocket.ork' in the '~/Documents/rockets/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create the spin stabilization report file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor open with a file named 'spin_stabilization_report.txt' located in the '~/Documents/exports/' directory.

task_completion.description: Document the cant angle and simulated apogee altitude in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the visible text in the report. It must explicitly mention the 3.0-degree cant angle and state a specific simulated apogee altitude.

task_completion.description: Explain spin physics and performance impacts in the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Read the visible text in the report. It must contain an explanation of how canted fins induce spin (e.g., aerodynamic forces creating a rolling moment) and note the performance impact compared to zero-cant (e.g., lower apogee, higher drag).

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the rocket and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that screenshots show the OpenRocket graphical interface being used to change the fin cant angle and run simulations, rather than the agent modifying the .ork XML file directly via terminal.

integrity.description: Apogee data in the report matches actual simulation results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the apogee altitude mentioned in the text report matches the simulation results visible in the OpenRocket 'Flight simulations' tab, ensuring the data wasn't hallucinated.

integrity.description: Report was saved as a genuine file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the report was written and saved to the specified directory using a text editor or standard file writing tools, rather than just echoing text to the terminal without creating the file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target cant angle is 3.0 degrees (acceptable tolerance is ±0.5 degrees, so 2.5 to 3.5 is valid). The modified rocket design must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/spin_stabilized_rocket.ork'. The report must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/spin_stabilization_report.txt'. The report must explicitly contain four elements: 1) the cant angle, 2) the simulated apogee altitude, 3) an explanation of how canted fins induce spin, and 4) observed performance impacts compared to a zero-cant configuration.
